caption:
Ferrari almost every season 

#formula1 #f1memes #scuderiaferrari #f1reels 

Since their last World ConstructorsвАЩ Championship in 2008, Scuderia Ferrari has established a reputation for remarkable but frustrating consistency, frequently finishing in second place.

Over the last two decades, the team has secured the runner-up spot in the ConstructorsвАЩ Championship at least seven timesвАФspecifically in 2012, 2015, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2022, 2024. This trend extends to their drivers; legends like Fernando Alonso (2010, 2012, 2013), Sebastian Vettel (2017, 2018), and Charles Leclerc (2022) have all narrowly missed the title while wearing the scarlet red.

caption:
F1 fans after the Japanese GP

#formula1 #f1memes #f1reels #fyp 
At the Japanese GP, Mercedes, McLaren, and Ferrari fans are ecstatic, celebrating a вАЬreturn to formвАЭ as all three iconic teams shared the podium in a show of pure competitive revival. ThereвАЩs a collective sense of relief and high energy, with supporters feeling their teams have finally mastered the 2026 regulations.

In contrast, Red Bull fans are devastated, watching their team tumble to a distant 8th place. The mood is one of shock and frustration, as the вАЬVerstappen eraвАЭ of dominance appears to have vanished, replaced by deep concerns over the teamвАЩs struggling new power unit.

caption:
Ferrari delivered both the fastest and second-fastest pit stops of the race

.
.
The 2026 Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka was a historic turning point for Formula 1, marked by 19-year-old Kimi AntonelliвАЩs masterclass performance that saw him become the youngest driver to ever lead the World DriversвАЩ Championship. Starting from pole, the Mercedes prodigy managed a chaotic race defined by a terrifying 50G crash from Oliver Bearman at Spoon Curve, which triggered a high-stakes Safety Car period. While the field grappled with the strategic demands of the new 2026 power unit regulations, Antonelli held off a surging Oscar Piastri and Charles Leclerc to secure his second career win

caption:
Everyone happy except Mercedes fans

.
.
From Suzuka perfection in 2025 to setting the tone for 2026 вАФ Max Verstappen proving that dominance isnвАЩt a moment, itвАЩs a mindset. рЯПБ

At the 2025 Japanese Grand Prix, Max Verstappen delivered one of his most complete performances. He secured pole position with an almost flawless qualifying lap, extracting maximum performance from the car through SuzukaвАЩs demanding high-speed corners. From the race start, he maintained control at the front, managing pace, tyres, and pressure without making any significant errors, even with strong competition close behind.

What made Verstappen stand out was his precision and consistency throughout the weekend. At a circuit where even the smallest mistake can cost time, he drove with total confidence, especially through the technical first sector. By converting pole into victory, he demonstrated not just speed but race intelligence and composure, reinforcing his reputation as one of the most dominant and reliable drivers on the grid
#formula1 #f1memes #f1 #racing
